translated: I'm exhausted, because of the love that I have, from my own Esau; for I think that a child is a person, and you will become a father together with children.

ref: “How is he?” Jacob asked. “He's fine,” they answered. “And here comes his daughter Rachel with the sheep.”
text: Egabia wo bena: Eli sesĩe mahã? Woɖo ŋu bena: Eli sesĩe! Kpɔ ɖa, via Raxel hã kplɔ alẽwo gbɔnae.
translated: What do you say: Is this true? You answer: It is true! Now, also with Raxel, the children are playing.

ref: “What do you want?” he asked in return. “The ring on that cord around your neck,” was her reply. “I also want the special walking stick you have with you.” He gave them to her, they slept together, and she became pregnant.
text: Eye wòbiae bena: Nuka matsɔ aɖo awɔba na wò mahã? Eɖo ŋu be: Wò ŋkɔsigɛ, si nètsɔna ɖoa ŋkɔ nu dzi, kple eŋuti ka kpakple wò atikplɔ, si le asiwò la! Tete wotsɔ wo nɛ, eye wòmia egbɔ, eye wòfɔ lu nɛ.
translated: And you say again: Now I see that you have been angry with me? He/she says: You are stubborn, you think you can do anything, and your words and thoughts that are in your heart! Now you will be humbled, you will be ashamed, and you will be punished.

ref: So the servant gave Abraham his word that he would do everything he had been told to do.
text: Eye dɔla la tsɔ eƒe asi ɖo eƒe aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe ali me, eye wòka atam nɛ ɖe nya sia dzi.
translated: And the money took his hand to Abraham’s farm, and you say that this is enough.

ref: Jacob answered: You've seen how hard I've worked for you, and you know how your flocks and herds have grown under my care.
text: Eɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Wò ŋutɔ ènya alesi mesubɔ wòe, kple alesi wò lãha va zu le gbɔnye.
translated: Thus it is said: If your hand is empty so that I may help you, and if you wish to go to the market.

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Kpekɔ sia kple dzesikpe la anye ɖase Nyemekpɔ mo ato kpekɔ sia kple dzesikpe sia ŋu ava gbɔwo o, eye wò hã mekpe mo aɖo tame vɔ̃ ato kpekɔ sia kple dzesikpe sia ŋu ava gbɔnye o!
translated: Today and tomorrow are not safe days for Nyemekpɔ to give you anything; you must also be careful again today and tomorrow to avoid danger!
